channel.title: Chroniq Now: Breaking Global News and Headlines Daily
channel.description: Chroniq Now - your trusted source for breaking global news, in-depth analysis and up-to-the-minute coverage of politics, business, culture and more.

item.title: KPMG to Cut 10% of US Audit Partners in Major Restructuring
item.description:
Accounting giant KPMG is laying off roughly 100 US audit partners after a voluntary retirement push failed to meet cost-cutting targets, marking one of the firm's most significant workforce reductions in recent memory.

Key points:
• Approximately 10% of KPMG's US audit partnership will be cut, affecting several dozen partners across its assurance practice
• Years of voluntary early retirement programs consistently failed to attract enough participation to meet the firm's restructuring goals
• The move comes under new CEO Tim Walsh, appointed nine months ago, who has been reshaping leadership within the audit and assurance division
• AI is increasingly automating key steps in the audit process, prompting Big Four firms to rethink staffing and partner headcount

Read the full article: KPMG to Cut 10% of US Audit Partners in Major Restructuring

item.title: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
item.description:
Driven Advisors and Administrative Services face scrutiny in a legal dispute with Bancrédito Holding Corp, owner of Bancrédito International Bank and Trust.

Read the full article: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
